LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MOTION TO STAY BY RESPONDENTS GERALD JONES, EMMETT COBB, JR., CHARLES HARRIS, ROZELLE LEE, JAMES MCCRAY, JR., DEREK TOLLIVER AND MARVIN NORMAN (COLLECTIVELY "JONES") IS GRANTED. JONES' REQUESTS FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE, WHICH ARE UNOPPOSED, ARE GRANTED. THE MOTION IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 128(A)(8), AS WELL AS THE COURT'S INHERENT AUTHORITY TO STAY AN ACTION WHEN APPROPRIATE (JORDACHE ENTERS. V. BROBECK, PHLEGER & HARRISON (1998) 18 CAL.4TH 739, 758). SEE DEPT. 302 MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS. JUDGE: JOSEPH QUINN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.

RESPONDENT GERALD JONES, EMMETT COBB, CHARLES HARRIS, ROZELLE LEE, JAMES MCCRAY, DEREK TOLLIVER, MARVIN NORMAN, JONES MEMORIAL HOMES INC., JONES SENIOR HOMES, INC.'s MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is Off calendar per the request of the moving party.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of April 7, 2016 Order Staying Matter
Off Calendar May-16-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Stephen Dye of Schnader, Harrison, Segal & Lewis LLP telephone number 415-364-6713 for Petitioners Gladys Perry and Robert Mitchell.
Dolores Dalton of Goldfarb & Lipman LLP telephone number 510-836-6336 for all Respondents.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PETITIONERS GLADYS PERRY and ROBERT MITCHELL'S Motion For Reconsideration Of April 7, 2016 Order Staying Matter is DENIED. The court lacks jurisdiction because Petitioners' motion is not "based upon new or different facts, circumstances." (See CCP 1008(a); see also CCP 1008(c) ["No application to reconsider any order or for the renewal of a previous motion may be considered by any judge or court unless made according to this section."].) The motion is denied on the alternative jurisdictional grounds that Petitioners motion violates section 1008 in that Petitioner failed to " state by affidavit what application was made before, when and to what judge, what order or decisions were made, and what new or different facts, circumstances, or law are claimed to be shown." (See CCP 1008(a); see also CCP 1008(c).) Respondents shall submit a proposed order consistent with this tentative. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Action
Off Calendar Apr-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Scott Tate of Schnader Attorneys at Law telephone number 415-364-6719 for Petitioners Gladys Perry and Robert Mitchell.
Dolores Dalton of Goldfarb Lipman LLP telephone number 510-836-6336 for all Defendants.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. The Motion to Stay by Respondents Gerald Jones, Emmett Cobb, Jr., Charles Harris, Rozelle Lee, James McCray, Jr., Derek Tolliver and Marvin Norman (collectively "Jones") is GRANTED. Jones' requests for judicial notice, which are unopposed, are GRANTED. The motion is granted pursuant to CCP section 128(a)(8), as well as the court's inherent authority to stay an action when appropriate (Jordache Enters. v. Brobeck, Phleger & Harrison (1998) 18 Cal.4th 739, 758). The instant petition raises the issue whether directors of Jones Memorial Homes, Inc. must be Church members. Petitioners Gladys Perry and Robert Mitchell (collectively, "Perry") rely on a 2006 by-law amendment that so provided; Jones argues that the 2006 by-law amendment is ineffective and unenforceable and, in any event, a 2015 by-law amendment provides that directors need not be Church members. (See Jones' Reply Declaration Ex. A at pp. 12, 25, 27.) The previous action Memorial United Methodist Church v. Jones Memorial Homes, Inc., Case No. CGC-14538144 addressed the issues whether the 2006 by-law amendments were effective and enforceable; that judgment is on appeal in Case No. A145711 ("Appeal"). Thus, to avoid unnecessary and duplicative adjudication, confusion and to eliminate the risk of inconsistent judgments, this matter is stayed pending resolution of the Appeal. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
